title: Domain Names
drDigitalIntro: A domain name is the core of a website's address. In google.com, 'google' is the domain name and '.com' is the extension. .com means commercial, .org means organization, .edu means education. Some sites use country codes: .uk for United Kingdom, .ca for Canada. Domain names are unique — only one website can own google.com.

playgroundTask.goal: Notice the different endings on web addresses

steps.say: google.com ends in .com — that means a company. Type google.com and press Enter.
wikipedia.org ends in .org — an organization, not a business. Type wikipedia.org and press Enter to see the different ending.

drDigitalSuccess: You visited a .com (company) and a .org (organization). The ending after the dot tells you what kind of site it is.
drDigitalHint: Type each address in the bar and press Enter. Watch the ending: .com vs .org.
